data.title: Exelixis downgraded to market perform by BMO Capital
data.description:
• BMO Capital Markets has downgraded Exelixis (NASDAQ:EXEL) to market perform from outperform as it sees limited upside for the stock in 2025.
  • The investment firm said it sees a balanced risk/reward for an upcoming readout for zanzalintinib in colorectal cancer, or CRC.
  • BMO now views the drug’s success in CRC at 50%, up from 35%, and has raised its price target to $40 from $36.
  • However, it also sees success of the trial "as a toss-up, given the history of cabometyx's (EXEL's first generation multi-kinase inhibitor) combination with Tecentriq (atezolizumab) in this indication.”

data.title: J.P. Morgan revises ratings on five REITs ahead of 2025
data.description:
J.P. Morgan revised its ratings on five real estate investment trusts in conjunction with the financial services firm's 2025 REIT outlook.

JPM upgraded the office REIT Kilroy Realty (NYSE:KRC) to Overweight from Neutral, and increased the 2025 year-end price target to $49 from $45. KRC was trading +5.86% Friday late morning to $41.19.

"We believe that over the course of 2025, leasing should improve in some of KRC's core West Coast markets and could prompt a re-rating in the shares," said analysts led by Michael Mueller in a research note.

The rating aligns with the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha authors rating of Buy. The Quant Rating system grades KRC as Strong Buy.

Retail REIT Federal Realty Investment Trust (NYSE:FRT) was also upgraded to Overweight from Neutral, with the price target increased to $125 from $122. FRT stock was up 2.07% to $111.82.

The ratings were raised largely considering "external growth (particularly acquisitions) will be a more meaningful driver of stock performance in 2025" and the relative valuation of the stock.

Meanwhile, J.P. Morgan lowered its recommendation on the retail REIT Kimco Realty (NYSE:KIM), health care REIT Healthcare Realty Trust (NYSE:HR), and industrial REIT Lineage (NASDAQ:LINE) to Neutral from Overweight.

KIM's visible external growth should be a more prominent stock driver in 2025, while HR's CEO search may overshadow the company's progress in the near-term.

KIM gets a Buy rating from the Wall Street community and SA analysts, and a Strong Buy from Quant. HR gets a Hold from the Street analysts, SA authors and Quant.

For LINE, JPM says, "We understand that the stock is already down significantly since its July IPO and subsequent highs, but weakness in throughput volumes will likely continue to be a marginal headwind for organic growth and our sense is that with its higher current equity costs, it may pull some of the company's/Street's focus away from external growth/acquisitions at the margin."

LINE is assigned a Buy by the sell-side analysts, a Hold by the SA authors and Sell by Quant.

data.title: Rigetti gets initiated with bullish views at Craig-Hallum
data.description:
Update: The story was updated with stock movement.

Quantum computing stocks have been receiving a lot of attention on Wall Street, with the latest action being from Benchmark, which raised D-Wave Quantum's (NYSE:QBTS) price target, and Craig-Hallum, which began coverage of Rigetti Computing (NASDAQ:RGTI) with a Buy rating.

Shares of D-Wave jumped about 20% on Friday, while Rigetti soared around 24%.

D-Wave Quantum: Benchmark's Analyst David Williams said they were increasing the price target to $8 from $3 following their meeting with D-Wave's CFO John Markovich. The sentiment for quantum has clearly shifted over the last few weeks, with multiple industry and company-specific announcements helping drive the price inflection, he added.

The analyst thinks a recent capital raise eliminates the risk discount driven by much liquidity and dilution concerns, which they viewed as the largest overhang to the stock. However, with both concerns now cleared and the raise considerably less dilutive than potentially expected, the analyst believes the D-Wave story has become significantly more attractive.

Williams said he remains encouraged by the strength of the company's new programs in the pipeline and expect the cadence of deployments to accelerate as the new go-to-market strategy ramps up.

The analyst noted that D-Wave's go-to-market has historically been through its quantum-as-a-service platform and Professional Services business but did not have a hardware sales component. However, the company has become increasingly open to moving in this direction, which could generate high single digit millions in revenue per unit.

On the government front, the recent "Awardable" designation by the Department of Defense, or DOD, Tradewinds should help accelerate procurement and broaden access to D-Wave's platform, supporting quantum annealing adoption across the military complex, according to the analyst.

Tradewinds is DOD's platform for sourcing, funding, and developing solutions to challenges in the artificial intelligence, machine learning, digital, and data analytics space.

Williams believes that the company is positioned to benefit from the National Quantum Initiative Reauthorization Act over the next five years and sees room for upside if the firm gains traction in hardware sales.

Rigetti Computing: Craig-Hallum initiated coverage of the Berkeley, Calif.-based company with a Buy rating and $12 price target.

Analysts led by Richard Shannon said quantum computing has an opportunity to revolutionize the world like very few technologies can, and though its commercial impact is still years in the future, its market size and disruptive potential for classical compute makes it relevant to investors today. Rigetti has unique scaling advantages which may allow it to achieve equivalency or superiority versus its direct peers, and to achieve quantum advantage in the next few years.

The analysts added that Rigetti and other quantum computing names, in the recent weeks, have been caught up in a retail-driven fervor.

Although this environment creates risks for investors in the near-term that the analysts do not downplay, but they provided other perspectives, such as — the transformative impact of Al was not recognized by the markets until all at once in the spring of 2023, and investors seem to be expecting the next elemental step in computing.

In addition, the analysts said that even after this run up, the $1B to $8B valued quantum computing companies are still small compared to the more than $1T market cap classical compute-driven companies that could be disrupted.

The analysts also said that there is scarcity value in the public markets for quantum computing plays. As such, they think Rigetti and quantum computing deserve a place in investors' portfolios today.

Shannon and his team noted that classical computing is a huge market, measured in trillions of dollars, and quantum computing can displace some of it, while serving new problems classical compute cannot, which shows that quantum computing addresses enormous markets.

The analysts added that because of its potential to displace this large classical computing market, the potential for mergers and acquisitions by trillion dollar market cap companies that are threatened is real. As the most mature U.S.-based pure play superconducting quantum computing company, Rigetti is uniquely positioned to benefit from this type of interest, the analysts noted.

On Thursday, D.A. Davidson began coverage of IonQ (IONQ) with a Buy rating and $50 price target. Separately, Benchmark raised the price target on IonQ's stock to $50 from $20.

Shares of quantum computing related stocks have surged this year. Year-to-date, Quantum Computing (QUBT) has skyrocketed over 1,500%, Rigetti Computing (RGTI) has soared over 650%, and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) has gained more than 600%. IonQ has added more than 200%, while Arqit Quantum (ARQQ) has jumped over 125%.

data.title: J.P. Morgan’s top Healthcare stocks for 2025
data.description:
J.P. Morgan’s U.S. equity analysts picked their top stocks for 2025, selecting “the most compelling investment ideas” across every sector.

These are the healthcare stocks (NYSEARCA:XLV) highlighted:

Vertex Pharmaceuticals Inc. (VRTX) – Price target: 503.00

Analysts say this is one of the highest quality names in their coverage and a core large-cap biotech stock “given a durable and dominant high margin cystic fibrosis franchise and progressing pipeline.”

Travere Therapeutics (TVTX) – Price target: 42.00

J.P. Morgan sees continued launch momentum for Filspari in IgAN medications and a positive outlook for products for kidney disease.

“We can comfortably see Travere (TVTX) shares doubling on regulatory progress [and] approval in the FSGS (focal segmental glomerulosclerosis, a kidney disease) indication,” analysts said.

Blueprint Medicines Corp. (BPMC) – Price target: 126.00

Since the company got its approval for Indolent Systemic Mastocytosis (or ISM) disease medicine Ayvakit, the company has increased its revenue run rate to about $500M annually.

“While we do expect seasonal choppiness in the coming quarters, we believe Ayvakit peak sales [puts the company] well on track towards $2B guidance,” analysts said. “With the launch… will see continued growth, also driven by favorable real world patient experiences to date.”

Xenon Pharmaceuticals (XENE) – Price target: 63.00

This company is poised to rally in 2025, according to J.P. Morgan.

“We anticipate phase 3 trials evaluating azetukalner as an adjunctive treatment in epilepsy to begin to read out in 2025, starting with topline X-TOLE2 results in focal onset seizures in [the second half of 2025.]”

“We see azetukalner as well positioned to see robust uptake in the adjunctive epilepsy space if the trials are positive, which we would expect.”

HealthEquity Inc. (HQY) – Price target: 110.00

HealthEquity is “our favorite near-term opportunity given the strong fundamental trajectory over the next 3 years, driven by HSA market penetration and higher interest rates relative to [years 2020-2022],” J.P. Morgan analysts said.

“Our model points to about 20% three-year EBITDA CAGR (FY24-FY27), with the company doubling its EPS over that period, driven by top-line growth and 700 basis points of margin expansion.”

Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) – Price target: 670.00

Thermo Fisher is still “best positioned to gain share across its end-markets behind its large commercial engine and superior portfolio.”

“We see a number of potential upside opportunities in 2025 for Thermo Fisher (TMO) such as China stimulus, biotech funding improvement, Biosecure Act related share gains in the CDMO (contract development and manufacturing organization), and faster acceleration in bioprocessing recovery.”

Cencora Inc. (COR) – Price target: 289.00

The pharmaceutical distributors are relatively well positioned compared to other areas in the healthcare world, analysts said. They are less exposed to regulatory scrutiny.

“Entering 2025, we see Cencora’s (COR) guidance demonstrating management’s continued strong execution, while the acquisition of Retina Consultants of America supports Cencora’s specialty strategy.”

“Given the underlying strength of the U.S. business, we believe Cencora (COR) is well positioned to continue executing along their longer-term operating targets as the company builds on momentum in expanding their capabilities to drive growth within attractive specialty and pharma markets.”

Boston Scientific Corp. (BSX) – Price target: 110.00

In 2024, the company “cemented itself in the elite double-digit sales growth class of large cap companies, with momentum to continue into 2025 with multiple shots on goal across the business,” analysts said.

Eli Lilly and Co. (LLY) – Price target: 1,100.00

This company is “the cleanest name in our sector.”

“The stock’s recent pullback has created a more attractive entry point for what has perpetually been an expensive story,” analysts said. “From here, we see a clear path for the stock to recover driven by a strong and highly defensible position in an unprecedentedly large T2D/Obesity market, strong core prescription trends for Zepbound/Mounjaro as capacity ramps and promotion efforts take hold and a number of clinical catalysts including ph3 data for Lilly’s oral GLP-1 orforglipron (for mid-2025).”

PTC Therapeutics Inc. (PTCT) – Price target: 74.00

“We see the Street undervaluing the >$1B peak sales potential for sepiapterin in PKU, benefited by improved efficacy and expanded breadth over incumbent commercial assets,” analysts said. “We see 2024’s trend of constructive FDA interactions continuing with votoplam as the company seeks to secure an accelerated approval path in Huntington’s disease, setting up for materially higher upside reaction to expanded 12-month PIVOT-HD results.”

J.P. Morgan sees its shares “further benefiting from a broader investor base, altogether outperforming the group in 2025.”

data.title: OpenAI plans to appeal €15M fine by Italian data protection regulator
data.description:
• OpenAI plans to appeal a €15M, or $15.59M, fine by Garante, Italy's data protection agency, which was issued to the startup on Friday for allegedly training ChatGPT without adequate legal basis or transparency.
  • The investigation focused on a period from November 2022 through March 2023. Since then, ChatGPT has launched its Privacy Centre, where users can opt out of their data being used for artificial intelligence training.
  • "The Garante's decision is disproportionate, and we will appeal," said an OpenAI spokesperson, in a statement to Seeking Alpha. "When the Garante ordered us to stop offering ChatGPT in Italy in 2023, we worked with them to reinstate it a month later."
  • "They've since recognised our industry-leading approach to protecting privacy in AI, yet this fine is nearly twenty times the revenue we made in Italy during the relevant period," the Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) company added. "We believe the Garante's approach undermines Italy's AI ambitions, but we remain committed to working with privacy authorities worldwide to offer beneficial AI that respects privacy rights."

data.title: JPMorgan spotlights 12 consumer names in its 'stocks for 2025' list
data.description:
JPMorgan's U.S. equity analysts have unveiled their "Stocks for 2025," highlighting some of their top investment ideas. These selections span growth, value, income, and short strategies, offering a diverse range of compelling ideas.

One area that was specifically highlighted was the consumer space, with the investment institution picking 12 companies in particular. Outlined below are those 12 stocks along with each of their price targets:

AerCap Holdings N.V. (AER): Price target $108.

Birkenstock Holding (BIRK): Price target $81.

BorgWarner Inc. (BWA): Price target $51.

Carvana (CVNA): Price target $300.

Post Holdings (POST): Price target $135.

Restaurant Brands International (QSR): Price target $80.

While JPMorgan didn’t specifically mention any exchange-traded funds, investors can still look to them to gain further exposure to the consumer space. Listed below are some popular consumer focused ETFs:

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (NYSEARCA:XLY), (VCR), (FXD), (FDIS), (RSPD), and (RXI).

Consumer Staples ETFs: (NYSEARCA:XLP), (VDC), (IYK), (FSTA), (KXI), and (RSPS).

data.title: Xometry rises 11% as Wedbush initiates coverage with Outperform rating
data.description:
Xometry (NASDAQ:XMTR) rose 11% as Wedbush initiated coverage on the stock with an Outperform rating as it sees the firm to be well-positioned to take share within the global custom manufacturing industry.

"We see a multi-year runway of relatively high growth versus digital marketplace peers," the analysts said in a note.

The firm's high growth is expected to be driven by rising adoption among both buyers and suppliers, deepening penetration with enterprise accounts, expanding access to a larger set of manufacturing capabilities, broadening services offered to suppliers, and the prospect of continued international growth, Wedbush said.

"The company more than doubled the number of buyers and suppliers on the platform over the past three years, and we see a long runway of growth as awareness rises and initiatives to increase spending among existing buyers take hold," the analysts added.

Shares in the company were up 13.5% since the start of the year.

SA analysts rate the firm at Buy, while SA quant rates it at Strong Buy.

Wedbush has set a price target of $48.

data.title: Global auto sales to reach 89.6M in 2025—S&P Global Mobility
data.description:
Global new light vehicle sales are expected to increase 1.7% year-on-year to 89.6 million units in 2025, according to a forecast by S&P Global Mobility.

The report added the global light vehicle sales for the full year of 2024 are anticipated to touch 88.2 million units, a rise of 1.7% from the prior year, helped by ongoing inventory restocking, as supply chains have become more steady.

In the U.S., the report expects sales volumes to reach 16.2 million units in 2025, which would be an increase of 1.2% from the projected 2024 level of 16 million units.

"2025 brings with it mixed opportunities and uncertainty for the auto industry as a new administration and policy proposals take hold," said Chris Hopson, manager of North American light vehicle sales forecasting for S&P Global Mobility.

"Vehicle pricing levels are expected to decline but remain high; interest rates are expected to shift further downwards, but inflation levels are anticipated to remain sticky, and new vehicle inventory should also progress, but careful management is expected too. Combined with an uneasy consumer, we project this translates to mild growth prospects for auto sales," Hopson added.

In Western/Central European market, the report expects sales are expected to come just under 15 million units in 2024, as customers continued to exercise caution.

"Into 2025, this storyline will intensify as strict 2025 emission rules further influence the market mix and topline, S&P Global Mobility forecasts the market flatlining around 15 million units, up by just 0.1% y/y," the report said about the European market.

In mainland China, for 2024, the sales are expected to at least 25.8 million, helped by the combination of the CNY130 billion extension of New Energy Vehicle incentives, and the new CNY75 billion trade-in scheme.

The report said the global light vehicle production is set to touch 89.1 million units, a 1.6% decrease as compared to the prior year. All the regions, except mainland China and South America experienced decline.

For next year, S&P Global Mobility expects global light vehicle production levels to decline by 0.4%, to 88.7 million units.

S&P Global Mobility sees global sales for battery electric passenger vehicles to post 15.1 million units for 2025.

Potentially relevant automotive stocks: Ford (F), General Motors (GM), Stalantis (STLA), Honda (HMC), Hyundai (OTCPK:HYMTF), Mazda (OTCPK:MZDAF), Nissan (OTCPK:NSANF), BMW (BMWYY), and Mercedes-Benz (OTCPK:MBGAF).

data.title: Biden administration to blacklist chip designer that ordered TSMC chip- report
data.description:
The Biden administration is moving to blacklist chip designer Sophgo, which illegally ordered a chip used in a Huawei AI processor, Reuters reported, citing an unnamed source.

Sophgo will be placed in the U.S. Commerce Department's Entity List. It is an affiliate of bitcoin mining equipment firm Bitmain.

The company was reportedly flagged for ordering a chip from Taiwan Semiconductor (NYSE:TSM) that matched one found on Huawei's Ascend 910B AI processor.

data.title: CAE Inc. gains as activist takes stake, wants to help with CEO search
data.description:
CAE Inc. (NYSE:CAE) rose 3% as an activist disclosed a stake and wants to help the flight simulator find a new chief executive officer.

Browning West LP accumulated a 4.3% stake in CAE, according to a letter the activist sent to the company's board on Friday.

"We are encouraged that the Board has taken the first steps toward recruiting a new CEO," Browning West partners Usman Nabi and Peter Lee wrote in the letter. "However, we believe CAE must recruit a proven CEO with a verifiable track record of value creation. We urge the Board not to act hastily in its CEO search, but rather to engage with us to collectively recruit the best possible leader."

CAE (CAE) announced last month that president and chief executive officer, Marc Parent, will be leaving the company at next year's annual general meeting in August 2025.
